submitted_payload.name: mistlight
submitted_payload.message_summary: Requested AI Dev Setup consider adding Ralph Workflow with Codeberg as primary repo, GitHub as mirror, and a finished-tested-ready-to-review positioning angle.
submitted_payload.suggested_listing_line: Ralph Workflow — free open-source workflow layer for unattended coding runs that aims to hand back finished, tested code ready to review.

verification.execution_method: Headless Playwright submission through the live Tally contact form embedded on the AI Dev Setup contact page.
verification.success_signal: Tally confirmation text returned: 'Thanks for your message!'

why_this_action: The latest audit says to pause another broad directory burst and another same-day curator batch, so the best fresh move is a single higher-intent coding-tool surface with a live executable contact path.
AI Dev Setup already targets AI coding / dev-tool discovery, which is closer to Ralph Workflow's strongest adoption story than another generic AI-tools directory.
The site exposes a working embedded contact form right now, so this could be executed immediately from the runtime instead of producing another handoff artifact.
The message kept Codeberg first and framed Ralph Workflow around finished, tested, ready-to-review outputs rather than generic orchestration language.
expected_outcome: A new developer-facing citation or listing path that routes qualified evaluators to the Codeberg repo first.
replacement_condition: If AI Dev Setup shows no reply, listing, or visible referral evidence by 2026-06-07, treat this as pending-only and use the next fresh-action slot on another executable high-intent workflow/coding surface instead of another generic contact replay.
